title: Gas report - Week 30
summary:
LNG
There was a little uptick in activity this week, especially in the Atlantic but that did not translate into higher rates for all the routes. There were a few ships that were on subjects released, which meant position lists were longer than anticipated and potential gains were not yet realised. In the Pacific, a few fixtures did drive rates positively but not in any great surge, which is to be expected during the summer months.

children.title: LPG
children.summary: With another quiet week for LPG across the board, there has not been much in a driving force for rates, summer doldrums have kept levels flat, and brokers are not reporting much fixing, let alone enquiry at the moment. The new acceptances expected at the beginning of the week did not do much to bolster the rates and as such we look forward to another weekend.
